refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and the operational context (agreed network conditions), ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify it remains within the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor network link quality to detect packet loss in real-time.
FR2: Implement error correction and packet retransmission mechanisms to reduce effective packet loss.
FR3: Provide feedback and alerts to teleoperation control systems when packet loss approaches limits.

candidate_dps: DP1: Real-time network quality monitoring module with packet loss detection algorithms.
DP2: Adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission capabilities.
DP3: Teleoperation control interface integration for feedback and alerting.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Combination of DP1, DP2, and DP3 to comprehensively address packet loss monitoring, mitigation, and operator feedback.

potential_sources: DP1 and DP2 tightly coupled due to real-time data exchange.
DP3 depends on DP1 and DP2 outputs but maintains clear interface boundaries.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DPs form a coupled but manageable set with well-defined interfaces, minimizing cross-domain interference.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters and stable enough for monitoring and correction.
Risk that error correction may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
Assumes teleoperation control system can process alerts without operator overload.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider specifying latency limits alongside packet loss to balance communication quality and responsiveness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation communication management, including network monitoring, error correction, and operator feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional communication channel over which teleoperation data and control signals are transmitted.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send network quality and packet loss status to the NGHE Solution for system-wide awareness.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands and task instructions from the NGHE Solution.

module_containment_scores.notes: Contains overall system coordination but less focused on teleoperation communication specifics.
Physical vehicle components have minimal direct involvement in packet loss management.
Highest containment score due to direct responsibility for teleoperation communication quality.
Safety Services focus on alerts and policies, not communication quality management.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the packet loss management FR to the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality functions, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overload of Teleoperation module if additional safety-related communication functions are added.
Dependency on Network Link interface reliability and performance.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor Teleoperation module complexity and consider splitting communication monitoring and control feedback if complexity grows.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR is clearly mapped to selected DPs that logically address packet loss monitoring, mitigation, and feedback.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as Teleoperation module encapsulates all related DPs.
Potential risk if Teleoperation module interfaces are not well defined or if Network Link interface degrades.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores strongly support Teleoperation as the appropriate IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by testing packet loss metrics and communication quality within the Teleoperation module using known interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FR to Teleoperation IM with well-defined interfaces ensures traceability and ownership.

recommended_revisions: Add latency constraints to FR to complement packet loss requirements.
Define fallback or degraded mode behavior in case packet loss exceeds limits.

decision.justification: The current level of functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment is consistent, well-contained, and verifiable with no major risks requiring immediate revision.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor network link quality to detect packet loss in real-time.
FR2: Implement error correction and packet retransmission mechanisms to reduce effective packet loss.
FR3: Provide feedback and alerts to teleoperation control systems when packet loss approaches limits.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; recommended revisions are for future refinement.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation control system can handle alerts without operator overload.
open_risks: Latency introduced by error correction may affect teleoperation responsiveness.
Potential complexity growth in Teleoperation module.
evidence_needed: Empirical packet loss and latency measurements under operational conditions.
Verification of alerting effectiveness and operator response.
